title: Diagnose a noisy-neighbor incident
description: A procedure to attribute degraded shared-pool performance to a specific tenant and contain it.
body: Use when latency degrades for many tenants at once in a shared pool. Do not scale blindly before finding the source. Steps: break down resource usage by tenant from per-tenant metrics; identify the tenant whose queries, connections, or jobs dominate; apply a temporary per-tenant limit; plan a durable fix such as a quota or dedicated resource. Validation signals include per-tenant metrics pinpointing the culprit and latency recovering after the limit. Risk boundary: throttling a tenant affects their service, so communicate and, for a persistent offender, escalate a move to dedicated capacity rather than silently degrading them.
